questionBank.question: The nth partial sum of sum a_n is:

choices.A: S_n = a_n
choices.C: S_n = a_n-a_{n-1}
choices.D: S_n = int a_n

questionBank.explanation: By definition, partial sum adds first n terms.
questionBank.timeTip: Write first few terms to avoid confusion.

questionBank.question: sum_{n=0}^infinity (1/3)^n converges to:

choices.D: Diverges

questionBank.explanation: Infinite geometric sum is 1/(1-r)=1/(1-1/3)=3/2.
questionBank.timeTip: Identify a and r carefully.

questionBank.question: sum 1/n^p converges when:

choices.C: p=1 only
choices.D: always

questionBank.explanation: p-series converges iff p>1.
questionBank.timeTip: Compare quickly to p=1 benchmark.

questionBank.question: Alternating series test requires:

choices.A: terms increase
choices.B: terms decrease to 0 in magnitude
choices.C: all terms positive
choices.D: ratio less than 1 always

questionBank.explanation: Need decreasing positive b_n and lim b_n=0.
questionBank.notesSnippet: Check monotonic decrease and limit zero.
questionBank.timeTip: Separate sign pattern from magnitude.

questionBank.question: If lim |a_{n+1}/a_n| = L < 1, then series:

choices.A: diverges
choices.B: converges absolutely
choices.C: conditionally converges
choices.D: inconclusive

questionBank.explanation: Ratio test with L<1 gives absolute convergence.
questionBank.timeTip: Interpret L in three cases: <1, >1, =1.

10.1.title: 10.1 Sequences and Partial Sums
10.1.content:
Sequence vs series

A sequence is list of terms. A series is sum of terms.

10.2.title: 10.2 Geometric and p-Series
10.2.content:
Fast tests

Geometric needs |r|<1. p-series converges iff p>1.

10.3.title: 10.3-10.5 Alternating + Ratio
10.3.content:
Common AP convergence tests

Alternating test, ratio test, and absolute vs conditional convergence.
